name: Endotracheal tube diameter
description: Internal diameter of the endotracheal tube in place, in mm (the tube size, e.g. 7.0, 7.5).

en.name: Endotracheal tube diameter
en.shortDescription: Internal diameter of the endotracheal tube in place, in mm (the tube size, e.g. 7.0, 7.5).
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context This concept captures the diameter of the endotracheal tube (ETT) in place — by convention the tube's internal diameter, the number used to size and label ETTs (e.g. 7.0, 7.5, 8.0). In source data it is a single numeric measurement per patient-timestamp, recording which tube size was used rather than a physiological measurement of the patient. It is documented in intensive care, anaesthesia, and emergency settings whenever a patient is intubated. It is reported in millimetres (**mm**) [1]. ## Included Concepts The set contains the single LOINC concept for endotracheal tube diameter: - *21491186 / 79562-5 — Endotracheal tube Diameter (LOINC)*: the internal diameter of the endotracheal tube, in millimetres. This is a leaf LOINC term with no descendants. **Note on units**: reported in **mm**, following the LOINC example unit for this term [1]. Standard adult ETT internal diameters fall roughly in the 7.0–8.0 mm range; paediatric and neonatal tubes are smaller. Tube sizes are commonly recorded in half-millimetre steps. ## Mapping Notes - This is the **tube's diameter**, not the tube device itself: the type of endotracheal tube is recorded separately (see the Endotracheal tube concept set). Map a recorded tube size here, and the device type (if any) to that other set. - **Internal diameter** is the conventional sizing figure for an ETT; if a source distinguishes internal from external diameter, only the internal diameter maps to this concept. - Tracheostomy tube sizing is a different device and is not captured by this concept. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 79562-5 — Endotracheal tube Diameter. Available: https://loinc.org/79562-5/

fr.name: Diamètre de la sonde endotrachéale
fr.shortDescription: Diamètre interne de la sonde endotrachéale en place, en mm (la taille de la sonde, ex. 7.0, 7.5).
